ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'iced tea' class (FOODON_03301103); no specific 'iced lemon tea' subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000AJ covers ready-to-drink tea broadly; no specific iced lemon tea code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for iced lemon tea beverage found in published SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one cup (250 ml / 250 g) of iced lemon tea as typically served at a Singapore hawker stall or casual restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon tea, ready-to-drink, sweetened — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo beverage category data for lemon tea
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact 'Season Ice Lemon Tea' branded entry exists in approved databases. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355 (Tea, ready-to-drink, lemon-flavored, sweetened) and cross-checked against HPB Singapore Foodo beverage category averages for sweetened lemon tea. The product sold at The Noodle Place is a standard sweetened iced lemon tea; nutrient profile is consistent with this category. Per-100g values reflect a lightly sweetened ready-to-drink lemon tea (~5–6 g sugar per 100 ml).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened tea and lemon tea beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for 'Season Ice Lemon Tea' exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 28 is derived from the closest analogous entry: sweetened ready-to-drink tea beverages, which consistently show GI values in the range 18–35 in the University of Sydney GI Database and in Atkinson et al. (2008). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (28 × 13.75) / 100 ≈ 3.9, where carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 5.5 × 2.5 = 13.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~4 mg per 100 ml; ~10 mg per 250 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Iced lemon tea is very low in sodium (~10 mg per standard 250 ml serving), making it acceptable for individuals managing hypertension, though added sugar content should be monitored if consumed frequently.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~5.3 g per 100 ml; ~13.3 g per 250 ml serving) contributing to glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index is low (GI ≈ 28), the added sugar in sweetened iced lemon tea (~13 g per 250 ml serving) contributes meaningful glycemic load and should be limited or replaced with an unsweetened or reduced-sugar version for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, and very low sodium; tea polyphenols may confer modest cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Iced lemon tea contains no fat or saturated fat and is very low in sodium, posing minimal cardiovascular risk; however, regular consumption of the sweetened version should be moderated due to added sugar, which is associated with adverse cardiometabolic outcomes at high intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced lemon tea (冰柠檬茶 / Teh Lemon Ais) is a widely consumed cold beverage in Singapore and across Southeast Asia, made by brewing black tea, chilling it, and combining it with lemon juice, sugar, and ice. The drink is characterised by a refreshing balance of mild tea bitterness, bright citrus acidity from fresh or concentrated lemon juice, and sweetness from added sugar or syrup. It is served cold, typically in a tall glass or plastic cup over ice. In Singapore's hawker culture, iced lemon tea is a staple accompaniment to noodle and rice dishes and is available at virtually every kopitiam, food court, and casual restaurant. The product 'Season Ice Lemon Tea' sold at The Noodle Place on Circular Road represents the standard commercial sweetened iced lemon tea format. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie beverage (~22 kcal per 100 ml) with negligible protein and fat. Its primary macronutrient is carbohydrate in the form of added sugar (~5.3 g per 100 ml). The glycemic index is low (GI ≈ 28) due to the dilute sugar concentration and the presence of tea polyphenols, which may modestly attenuate postprandial glucose response. Sodium content is very low (~4 mg per 100 ml), making it suitable for individuals managing hypertension. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the added sugar content (~13 g per 250 ml serving) warrants moderation. Tea polyphenols (catechins, theaflavins) present in the black tea base may confer modest antioxidant and cardioprotective benefits. The beverage is naturally free of fat, cholesterol, gluten, and dairy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in iced lemon tea?
Is iced lemon tea good for diabetics?
Sugar content of ice lemon tea Singapore
Is iced lemon tea suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact branded nutritional data for 'Season Ice Lemon Tea' from The Noodle Place exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355 (sweetened ready-to-drink lemon tea) and cross-checked against HPB Singapore Foodo beverage category averages. The nutrient profile is consistent and defensible for a standard sweetened iced lemon tea, but exact formulation (sugar concentration, tea strength, lemon proportion) is not publicly disclosed by the restaurant.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero — iced lemon tea contains no protein-bearing ingredients in meaningful quantities; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355. 2. fat_g is naturally zero — this beverage contains no fat by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero as this is a fat-free beverage. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — no fibre-containing solids are present in the liquid beverage. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero — plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero — no vitamin D source present. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source for this beverage category. 8. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. 9. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. 10. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. 11. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. 12. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. 13. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. 14. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. 15. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. 16. glycemic_index set to 28 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sweetened ready-to-drink tea category); gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). 17. The food_name has been normalised from the menu name 'Season Ice Lemon Tea' to the canonical English food name 'Iced Lemon Tea'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases as 'Season Ice Lemon Tea'. 18. Cuisine classified as Singaporean based on the food's ubiquitous presence in Singapore hawker and kopitiam culture; multilingual names provided in English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per G6 Singaporean dish rules.
